content.full_text:
Linda Hedges,
Planning Commissioner
City of Victorville
14219 Deer Trail Drive
Victorville, CA 92392
August 13, 1991

Attn: Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Ms. Breeze,

Thank you for our conversation of today in which you confirmed that a
"Conflict of Interest", does not exist related to the following
situation. I am seeking a written confirmation from the FPPC on this
matter, and hope the Commission can issue it's ruling on this quickly.
The situation is as follows:

I am an appointed Victorville City Planning Commissioner. My
livelihood is earned as a salaried employee of a nonprofit, tax exempt
corporation in the City, where I run a day treatment center for
mentally ill adults.

In 1988 I founded another nonprofit, tax exempt corporation,
economically unrelated to the first. This second nonprofit corporation
has the mission to create permanent housing for the mentally ill
homeless, to be located somewhere in this County's unincorporated area
where the correct rural zoning will permit a ranch concept. The ranch
will not be in Victorville's jurisdiction or sphere of influence.

I am currently President of the Board of the second non-profit
corporation mentioned and receive no financial compensation of any type
for this activity, and never have. My husband and I serve as Volunteers
with this nonprofit corporation.

A builder who has been, and is, very active in Victorville has acted
as an informal consultant to the Board of the second non-profit
corporation since it's inception in 1988. This builder is now
interested in contributing to the second non-profit in a material way,
such as through a donation of money, materials or offset costs.

Would a donation by the builder, to a nonprofit with which I am
involved as a Volunteer, set up a potential Conflict of Interest
between myself and his applications before the Planning Commission?

[The image shows a mostly blank or heavily faded document page. The text is extremely difficult to read due to the age and condition of the paper. Only very faint marks and possible text impressions are visible, but they are too illegible to transcribe with any accuracy.]

It is my opinion that no conflict would exist as there is no material
benefit to me, my family or to any "business entity" with which I am
involved. Gov. Code 82002 states clearly "a nonprofit corporation is
not a business entity", and also clarifies that there is no "economic
interest" if to business entity I am benefitted by, as is involved.
The builder would be benefitting a nonprofit with which I am
associated as a Volunteer, but his donation would not be benefitting me,
my family, or my financial status in any material way.

By telephone you did concur with my views on this mamy matter and
suggested I might want to get this in writing, as I am now requesting.
Additionally, my misconception in our City seems to be that a Conflict
of Interest exists when a person such as myself, I speaks out publicly in
support of issues or a class of people, such as the homeless, and then
participates in making decisions which influence services to that group
of persons.

I maintain that I or my family or a "business entity" I am associated
with, would have to be materially benefitting from such votes, for a
Conflict to exist. Am I correct in assuming that an advocacy stand is
not the basis of Conflict of Interest, but that the basis of Conflict
is an economic benefit to the voting Commissioner?
Thank you for your speedy consideration of this matter and I will be
looking forward to your written response.

[signature]
Linda Hedges

cc: Councilwoman Peggy Sartor
    City Manager Jim Cox
    Ira Norris, Inco Homes
    City Attorney Henry Kraft

This image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with a beige/cream colored background. There is no visible text content to transcribe. The page shows some horizontal fold lines or creases but no legible written or printed text.
